hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 4, J. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 3. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — 9. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: A K has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
4 3 has little reason to lead the river here — two pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
Q J has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 6 10

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
